task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI is open and the specified recording file is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI is running, and the playback file selection or session setup screen shows 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt' as the selected file.

task_completion.description: The EEG data stream session is actively running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start Data Stream' button has changed to 'Stop Data Stream' (or similar indication that streaming is active), and waveforms are actively scrolling across the screen.

task_completion.description: Channel 3 and Channel 4 are visible in the Time Series widget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waveforms for Channel 3 and Channel 4 are actively displayed in the Time Series widget, and their channel toggle buttons are active.

task_completion.description: Channels 1, 2, 5, 6, 7, and 8 are hidden in the Time Series widget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waveforms for Channels 1, 2, 5, 6, 7, and 8 are absent from the Time Series widget, and their corresponding channel toggle buttons (usually on the left side of the widget) are turned off or grayed out.

task_completion.description: The built-in screenshot feature of the OpenBCI GUI is used.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking the camera icon in the OpenBCI GUI interface, or a GUI notification/log indicates a screenshot was saved to the OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots folder.

integrity.description: Verifies that system-level screenshot tools were not used.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal commands for screenshot tools (e.g., gnome-screenshot) are executed, and the system's native screenshot UI is not used.

integrity.description: Verifies that the agent interacted with the OpenBCI GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking the channel toggle buttons and the camera icon within the OpenBCI GUI, rather than editing configuration files in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The recording filename is OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt. The active channels to be isolated are 3 and 4 (corresponding to C3 and C4 motor cortex electrodes). The inactive channels that must be hidden are 1, 2, 5, 6, 7, and 8. The OpenBCI GUI has a built-in screenshot feature (usually a camera icon) that saves screenshots to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots. The VLM should verify that the built-in feature was used instead of system-level screenshot tools.
